title: git run manifest not known on podman run
body:
### Issue Description

Hi All 
When I  build an image tagged ```mycompany.registry/mydir/myapplication:latest``` and  then try to run 
podman run mycompany.registry/mydir/myapplication


I am getting this error
```
Trying to pull mycompany.registry/mydir/myapplication...
Error: initializing source docker:mycompany.registry/mydir/myapplication: reading manifest latest in  mycompany.registry/mydir/myapplication : manifest unknown: The named manifest is not known to the registry.
```
I have to either push the image to the registry or pass it the image ID instead.
Shouldnt podmand check local first before going out to remote registry?

comments.body:
I was passing in this in  ```--platform linux/amd64```   during  the run command, and if I remove it, it runs. 
I am building with it so what should be the case?

@powerserg19 did you specify `--platform linux/amd64` while building the image ? cause your `podman` VM  is runnning on `arm64` not `amd64`.  See output of your `podman info`

Since in case of `podman-remote` the actual image which is built is for `arm64` on the remote side and not on the client side. You can verify by inspecting the image before running it.

In layman terms:
* Your client is amd64
* Podman is running inside VM which is `arm64`
* Podman is building image for host architecture i.e `arm64`.
* While running you are specifiying `amd64` which is not present.

I hope this helps. Please feel free to re-open the issue if something is unresolved.
